title: TLS 1.3, Certificate Identity, ALPN, and HTTPS · Runtime Contract: Https Non Guarantees
summary: A service enables TLS without certificate-name validation, chooses HTTP after reading encrypted application bytes, allows unsafe versions, and treats encryption as authorization and content integrity proof. Planned artifact: Add and verify the https non guarantees increment in TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State that TLS protects a connection hop but does not provide application authorization, safe methods, trusted intermediaries, malware safety, or end-to-end storage secrecy.
Define supported versions, cipher and curve policy, session resumption, client authentication, and replay boundaries while treating early data explicitly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Https Non Guarantees · Theory Https Non Guarantees
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4394 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTPS on certificate identity, TLS policy, ALPN, and explicit early-data review; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Https Non Guarantees contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Https Non Guarantees · Theory Https Non Guarantees.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state that tls protects a connection hop but does not provide application authorization, safe methods, trusted intermediaries, malware safety, or end-to-end storage secrecy. For Predict Https Non Guarantees · Theory Https Non Guarantees, keep Https Non Guarantees connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
